feedback.text: I am fully committed to addressing Google’s control constraints for some time. We have just had to move Apple, and now we now have the next conclusion on open source software development, like in general a kind of controlled, large-scale old man over what you are installing, well noticed on a smartphone you bought and paid for, and restricts your own judgement, even though you have been growing for more than 15 years and have to change yourself to do what you have always done for you. (Apps sideloaden) I chose Android because I knew what I could expect; a self-determined exercise of my intention without someone who looks to me on the finger and can take the mobile phone out of my control if an app does not fit him into the picture. The boat-loading guidelines went far enough, and what has happened here is a change to Google’s monopolistic monopolies under the pretext of making conditions safer for everyone. Yes clearly, everything for protection only (google’s). We cannot be in favour of the cat, otherwise all the actions for the iPhone within the EU (which is probably being circumvented) would have been for the cat, during which the bird (even in this case) was completely shooting off. We cannot agree with what is happening here, I’m doing where I can disprove the project or be better able to prevent it. We are calling back our right to own and control as a final consumer! Windows does not impose any programmes on me, nor does my pizabäcker decide whether I eat or elsewhere.
